title: GAO flags satellite costs, launch risks in Space Force portfolio
summary: The GAO has highlighted rising costs and launch risks for missile-warning satellites in the Space Force portfolio, citing gaps in digital engineering and workforce reductions that could delay national security launches.

title: Pegasus launches Swift reboost mission
summary: A Pegasus XL rocket launched a mission to reboost NASA's Swift astrophysics spacecraft, potentially marking the final flight of the Pegasus rocket. The launch was reported by SpaceNews.

title: Chinese satellite manufacturer Hongqing raises $191 million
summary: Hongqing Technology, a satellite manufacturing affiliate of Landspace, raised $191 million in one of the largest funding rounds for a Chinese commercial satellite maker.

title: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies shifts focus to space
summary: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies is shifting its focus from rooftop applications to space, aiming to commercialize thin-film solar panels for orbital data centers and large satellite constellations.

title: Isar Aerospace to launch German-built Planet imaging satellite
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a contract with Planet’s German subsidiary to launch an imaging satellite, showcasing Germany's end-to-end space capabilities. This deal highlights the growing collaboration between European space companies and commercial satellite operators.

title: Polish space tech company Sybilla Technologies secures funds to enter U.S. market
summary: Sybilla Technologies, a Polish space tech company, secured a $10 million investment from BGK and 3TS Capital Partners to expand into the U.S. market. The funding aims to support the company's growth and international presence.

title: Making satellite communications more resilient
summary: Kymeta's Ryan Stevenson discusses the advantages and challenges of integrating multi-orbit and multi-band connectivity in a single satellite communications terminal with SpaceNews' Mike Gruss.

title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: The final Atlas 5 rocket launched on July 2, carrying a payload of Amazon's Leo satellites. This mission marked the last time the Atlas 5 vehicle would carry a satellite payload.

title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A report attributes Boeing's CST-100 Starliner issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's limited oversight. The findings highlight systemic challenges in the spacecraft's development.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC will vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications amid growing constellation plans. This initiative seeks to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' for satellite approvals.

title: 10 best Spanish cities to see the total solar eclipse 2026
summary: The news highlights the best Spanish cities to witness the total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, including A Coruña, Bilbao, Madrid, and Zaragoza. It provides a list of locations for optimal viewing.

title: America 250: From 1776 to the moon and beyond (A Space.com series)
summary: A Space.com series reflects on U.S. space achievements since 1776 and explores future possibilities as America celebrates its 250th anniversary.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 217 — America in Space
summary: The 217th episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik discussing the evolution of American space efforts.

title: Human flight was still 7 years away in 1776. Now, we're headed back to the moon
summary: The article reflects on humanity's progress in flight since 1776, noting that human flight was still decades away at that time. It highlights the modern ambition to return to the moon.

title: What did the night sky look like on the 1st Independence Day 250 years ago?
summary: The news discusses what the night sky looked like on July 4, 1776, during the U.S. Independence Day. It highlights what Benjamin Franklin and other colonists would have observed when they looked up at the sky.

title: How to find Uranus this week, the hardest planet I've ever tried to see
summary: This week offers a rare opportunity to spot Uranus in the night sky, aided by Mars' proximity. The event provides a great chance for skywatchers to observe the seventh planet.

title: As 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' turns 35, it's time to accept the truth: Terminator shouldn't be back
summary: As 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' turns 35, critics and fans reflect on the franchise's decline. The future of the Terminator series is uncertain.

title: Could humans someday explore Saturn's moon Titan, or will humanoid robots do it for us?
summary: The article discusses the potential for humans or AI-powered humanoid robots to explore Saturn's moon Titan in the future. It raises the question of whether humans will set foot on Titan or if robots will undertake the mission instead.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved multiple milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar exploration and base development.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company is working to restore the launch site to operational status.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A mission to rescue the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final Atlas satellite launch. Several orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to conduct an air launch for the Swift Boost Mission to extend the lifespan of a NASA space telescope. This marks the first mission for the Pegasus XL since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, which will aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data supports research into galactic structure and star formation.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Meanwhile, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 ministerial meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. The events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential applications. It suggests this innovation could significantly impact future space missions and robotic operations.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry, but lacks specific details.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have strengthened its partnerships with Middle Eastern space companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for collaboration in the space industry.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC, which had requested the images within 72 hours.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, Aurora. The mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The approach aims to minimize the need for Earth-based materials by utilizing lunar dust for structural support and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and revealed new contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims to achieve monthly lunar landings using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It provides an overview of payloads and their integration into these missions.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into the space applications sector.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Esrange Space Center in Sweden by 2028 under a new franchise model. The collaboration with SSC Space marks Firefly's expansion into international launch markets.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has achieved sufficient satellite deployment to enable initial service across key latitudes following a ULA launch. This marks a significant milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to deploy one of its next-generation Pelican satellites, with the launch potentially occurring as early as late 2026.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intent to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition marks a significant move in the industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully conducted a test call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation for emergency services connectivity. The test is part of efforts to enhance communication for first responders.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the applications dominating satellite internet traffic and their significance for non-governmental use. It highlights the growing importance of satellite networks as thousands of new satellites are launched into orbit.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless operations after spectrum transactions. The move aims to streamline the company's financial restructuring efforts.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO country for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: Unseenlabs has ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The contract was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president to oversee daily operations and expand manufacturing capabilities. Campbell will focus on driving growth and investments for the company.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of NASA's efforts to build a lunar base. These missions are critical for supporting future human and robotic exploration on the lunar surface.
